steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The main clause Ellen Ochoa left her position is complete and in past tense. The blank connects this clause to the noun phrase the space agency's astronaut training program. When a completed action is followed by its intended purpose, Standard English uses an infinitive (to + base verb), not a conjugated verb form.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to join': Infinitive of purpose — correctly expresses why Ochoa left her position; no subject is needed after a completed clause. ✓
  • Choice B — 'is joining': Violates verb tense agreement — present progressive clashes with the past-tense main clause and creates a run-on without a conjunction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'joined': Violates sentence structure — inserting a second past-tense verb creates a comma splice or run-on rather than expressing purpose. ✗
  • Choice D — 'joins': Violates verb tense agreement — present tense clashes with past-tense left and again creates a run-on. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
